description_long: This is a roughly cylindrical, hand-formed clay vase with an uneven, organic shape and a wide, flared rim. The surface is matte and textured, showing natural variations in color from earthy brown to reddish tones, with some lighter patches and visible imperfections. The rim is thick and irregular, suggesting it was shaped by hand rather than on a wheel. There are visible marks and chips along the body, and some areas appear to have been abraded or weathered over time. The interior is also unfinished and rough, consistent with traditional or ancient pottery techniques. Faint markings or numbers are visible on one side, possibly for cataloging or identification purposes.
description: A hand-formed, rustic clay vase with a wide, uneven rim and a rough, weathered surface in earthy brown and reddish tones.
description_view_0: The vase's rim appears thick and uneven, with a prominent bulge and visible chips on the lower body.
description_view_1: This view highlights the flared rim and a large, lighter patch on the body, with more pronounced surface texture.
description_view_2: A label or number is visible inside the rim, and the reddish coloration is more prominent on this side.
description_view_3: Faint red markings or writing are visible on the upper body, with more surface irregularities and chips.

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
